raid report, seizure memos, exhibits, and the appellant. In his 
cross-examination, the panch witness deposed that he did not 
hear the conversation regarding the demand and payment of 
the excess Rs. 2/-. He admitted that the currency notes were 
lying on the register when he reached the spot and were not 
recovered from the body or clothes of the accused. 
49.3 The IO deposed that after leaving the ACB office, he stayed 
behind while the Raid Officer, the complainant, the panch 
witness and others proceeded for the raid. At around 7:15 PM, 
the Raid Officer called him to the location and handed over 
the documents. In cross-examination, the witness admitted 
that the seizure memo does not mention that an attachie, 
sale register, and various stamp papers were seized from the 
appellant’s seat. He also admitted that he was not present
